articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: Mexico draws with Uzbekistan despite Raul Jimenez brace
article.description: Mexico allowed a 92nd minute equalizer from Otabek Shukurov that Uzbekistan a surprising 3-3 draw in a friendly on Tuesday in Atlanta, Georgia.
article.linkText: Jimenez brace wasted in Mexico, Uzbekistan draw

images.name: Mexico celeb Jimenez vs Uzbek_230912 [1296x729]
images.caption: Raul Jimenez celebrates after scoring a goal for Mexico against Uzbekistan.

article.story:
Mexico allowed a 92nd minute equalizer from Otabek Shukurov that handed Uzbekistan a surprising 3-3 draw in a friendly on Tuesday in Atlanta, Georgia.

Initially down 1-0 after a well-placed header from Uzbekistan's Bobir Abdixolikov in the 18th minute, Mexico quickly responded with a goal just three minutes later from Fulham striker Raúl Jiménez. Following a pass forward from Edson Álvarez and a poorly-timed shot from Roberto Alvarado, Jimenez pounced on the loose ball and slotted into the back of net to level the score.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Despite having more shots and a strong majority of possession in the first half, El Tri would slip up once again, allowing Uzbekistan's Azizbek Turg'unbaev to fire home a nice finish moments before the break in the first minute of injury time.

After some tactical changes and roster adjustments in the second half from manager Jaime "Jimmy" Lozano, Mexico began to claw their way back in the final stages of the friendly.

With more attack-minded players like Uriel Antuna, Jordi Cortizo and Santiago Giménez on the pitch, Mexico created dangerous opportunities late into the friendly, eventually leading to another equalizer from Jimenez. Scooping up a poor headed clearance from Uzbekistan, Jimenez tapped the ball into the back of the net, pushing Mexico to a 2-2 draw by the 81st minute.

Lozano's side did well to increase their attacking pressure, later leading to what appeared to be a possible game-winner from Antuna, who deflected a clearance from the opposing defense into the net in the 88th minute.

Unfazed by the goal, Uzbekistan pressed high up the field and earned another injury time scoring chance. With Shukurov standing over the ball for a free kick next to Mexico's 18-yard box, the midfielder launched his shot to the top left corner, giving Uzbekistan a dramatic comeback in the 92nd minute.

"It's a bitter taste. That's the reality because we had already earned the turnaround, I believe with passion, with heart," said Giménez to TUDN after the match.

"We have to be more self-critical and realize that we didn't play a good game," also stated fellow striker Jiménez. "There are many aspects that we need to continue improving. Jimmy's [Lozano] idea is there, you just have to hone it well."

The result leaves Mexico with a pair of draw in the international break for Lozano and his players, after Saturday's 2-2 final against Australia in Dallas.

As for Uzbekistan, the 2023 CAFA Nations Cup finalists have closed out their international window with a loss and tie in hand after losing 3-0 by the United States over the weekend.

Hours before Tuesday's match, Mexico announced a Dec. 16 friendly against Colombia in L.A.'s Memoria's Coliseum. The match will be Mexico's 15th game on U.S. soil in 2023 across all competitions.
